title: leontodon hispidus subsp. hastilis
intro: Leontodon hispidus subsp. hastilis, also known as Rough Hawksbeard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, including diarrhea and stomach ulcers. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Rough Hawksbeard can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, with some herbalists also using it in salves and creams to take advantage of its topical applications.
